refinement_sections.content: We need to figure out how to test different CPU scheduling algorithms to see which one works best for a new multi-core processor. This means we have to compare at least three algorithms and see how they perform with different types of work.

To do this, we will pick three CPU scheduling algorithms to test. I think we can use Round Robin, First-Come-First-Serve, and Shortest Job Next. Then, we will run these algorithms on some test programs that mimic real use cases.

We need to choose workloads that are common for the company. This could include things like running a web server, database operations, and some batch processing tasks. We should pick a mix of these to get a good comparison.

We should measure how fast each algorithm responds to tasks (response time), how many tasks it can complete in a given time (throughput), and how fair it is to all tasks (fairness). I think we can just look at these three metrics.
I assume that the workloads we choose will represent the actual tasks that the company does. Also, I assume that the tests will run in a similar environment to what they use in real life.
